title: French industry turns to war for jobs
subhed: As layoffs and plant closures spread across the automotive sector, defense, which was once criticized on moral grounds, is now seen as a new promised land for both manufacturers and workers.

France revives Gratitude Train for the US's 250th anniversary
subhed: A restored railcar from northern France will retrace the journey of the Gratitude Train, which carried gifts from Le Havre to New York in 1949.

title: Germany's Zverev wins French Open
subhed: Alexander Zverev beat Italy's Flavio Cobolli 6-1, 4-6, 6-4, 6-7 (5), 6-1 for his first title at Roland Garros on Sunday.

title: With 'En Nous,' Juliette Binoche dives into the heart of choreographic creation
subhed: The actress shares the journey she experienced in 2007 with dancer and choreographer Akram Khan and the creation of 'In-I,' performed 120 times globally. Now, after editing 200 hours of footage in 2023, 'En Nous' marks her first feature film as a director.

title: Working-class landmarks in the Paris region are being taken over by luxury and art powers
subhed: Projects at Le Large, built on a historic Renault factory site on Seguin island, or at the Maison du Peuple in Paris's western and northwestern suburbs, illustrate how major corporations are taking over former industrial sites.

Mélenchon holds first campaign rally in Saint-Denis as part of identity-based 'new France' strategy
subhed: The election of Bally Bagayoko and Aly Diouara as mayors in the Paris-region department of Seine-Saint-Denis in March were symbolically significant for Jean-Luc Mélenchon. Since 2018, the radical-left leader has made the area a testing ground for his 'new France' concept, as he is counting on the vote of its working-class citizens from immigrant communities for next year's presidential election.

title: Bernadette Chirac, the wife of ex-president Jacques Chirac, has died
subhed: An unfailing supporter of her husband's career, Bernadette Chodron de Courcel became a key figure a French politics. She was elected local councilor, a rare feat for a 'wife of,' and was very involved in charity work. She died at the age of 93.

title: Marjane Satrapi, powerful and outspoken artist who swore to 'stay true to herself'
subhed: Famous for her four-part graphic novel 'Persepolis' recounting her childhood in Iran during the Islamic Revolution, which she also adapted for the screen, Satrapi was also later involved with the Woman, Life, Freedom movement.
